original text:
The day we never dreamed about is here and well, it is a nightmare. The Microsoft Nokia deal is finally done with and the first changes are starting to trickle in. The first major change that Microsoft has brought in is nothing short of unthinkable - it has taken the Nokia name off the door.
A leaked letter from Nokia's headquarters in Finland shows that the Nokia Oyj division will now be called the Microsoft Mobiles Oy division.
"Please note that upon the close of the transaction between Microsoft and Nokia, the name of Nokia Corporation/Nokia Oyj will change to Microsoft Mobile Oy. Microsoft Mobile Oy is the legal entity name that should be used for VAT IDs and for the issuance of invoices." States the letter.
So, is Nokia actually out of the picture for good? Maybe not, but the Nokia brand name might be. Microsoft has brought the rights to the Nokia brand name for the next 10 years.
It looks pretty grim for Nokia fans right now.
Here is the full letter to add to your dismay.
New Bank Accounts: Please note that Microsoft Mobile Oy is going to have new bank accounts, for further information please contact us via AP  (registration required).
(IAS): (No registration required), or contact us via AP  (Registration required).
processed_text:
The day we never dreamed about is here and well, it is a nightmare.
The Microsoft Nokia deal is finally done with and the first changes are starting to trickle in.
The first major change that Microsoft has brought in is nothing short of unthinkable - it has taken the Nokia name off the door.
A leaked letter from Nokia's headquarters in Finland shows that the Nokia Oyj division will now be called the Microsoft Mobiles Oy division.
"Please note that upon the close of the transaction between Microsoft and Nokia, the name of Nokia Corporation/Nokia Oyj will change to Microsoft Mobile Oy.
Microsoft Mobile Oy is the legal entity name that should be used for VAT IDs and for the issuance of invoices."
States the letter.
So, is Nokia actually out of the picture for good?
Maybe not, but the Nokia brand name might be.
Microsoft has brought the rights to the Nokia brand name for the next 10 years.
It looks pretty grim for Nokia fans right now.
Here is the full letter to add to your dismay.
New Bank Accounts: Please note that Microsoft Mobile Oy is going to have new bank accounts, for further information please contact us via AP  (registration required).
(IAS): (No registration required), or contact us via AP  (Registration required).

original text:
Nomad Factory's Motown Retro Bundle is now on sale at only $39 until November 27th, a huge saving of $90 off the list price! The Motown Retro Bundle recreates the incredibly warm sound qualities of legendary analog hardware passive equalizers used by Motown engineers.
Designed to improve the quality of digital sound recordings. The plug-ins provide a simple and functional Vintage/Retro-Style interface, as well as low CPU consumption for lightening-fast processing. It add the Motown vibe to your mix.
The Retro Music-Tone is a 7-band passive equalizer plug-in emulation of the hardware version used exclusively by Motown engineers. Like the original hardware units, we have recreated the proportional Q which means that there is no adjustable Q bandwidth: small boost/cuts have a very broad Q and greater boost/cuts have a narrower one. You just have to adjust the gain of each fixed frequencies (from -8dB to +8dB) according to taste and the resulting tonal color.
processed_text:
Nomad Factory's Motown Retro Bundle is now on sale at only $39 until November 27th, a huge saving of $90 off the list price!
The Motown Retro Bundle recreates the incredibly warm sound qualities of legendary analog hardware passive equalizers used by Motown engineers.
Designed to improve the quality of digital sound recordings.
The plug-ins provide a simple and functional Vintage/Retro-Style interface, as well as low CPU consumption for lightening-fast processing.
It add the Motown vibe to your mix.
The Retro Music-Tone is a 7-band passive equalizer plug-in emulation of the hardware version used exclusively by Motown engineers.
Like the original hardware units, we have recreated the proportional Q which means that there is no adjustable Q bandwidth: small boost/cuts have a very broad Q and greater boost/cuts have a narrower one.
You just have to adjust the gain of each fixed frequencies (from -8dB to +8dB) according to taste and the resulting tonal color.

original text:
Peter Tse, Business Manager, Sustainable Construction Group, BSRIABSRIA is delighted to again be supporting the Offsite Construction Awards which celebrate the best in precision building design and delivery. The winners will be announced on Tuesday 6th March 2018 at ExCel - London. BSRIA is sponsoring the best use of MEP (mechanical electrical plumbing)Technology category. Deadline for submission is Friday 19th January.
Now in its fourth year, offsite manufacture removes much of the construction process from the construction site, leading to far-reaching and vital benefits. Across key sectors of UK construction, the awards will showcase innovation, celebrate best practice and recognise overall expertise in offsite construction through landmark projects, influential people and material and manufacturing excellence in this dynamic arena.
"These awards are a fantastic opportunity to recognise innovation - especially at a time when the construction industry is looking for unique and fresh solutions to address the challenges and opportunities arising from the sector's commitment to delivering more, better quality new homes.
BSRIA supports forward thinking and finding complementary ways to provide a step change in delivery and performance of the built environment. The awards will provide a glimpse into what is achievable and presents a unique opportunity to celebrate the best in precision building design and delivery.
New categories have been added for 2018 and include: housing project of the year; best use of POD technology; product innovation award; offsite pioneer of the year; and engineer of the year. This is a great opportunity where industry specialists get the chance to showcase their inspirational achievements first hand to a panel of leading industry figures.

This is a great opportunity where industry specialists get the chance to showcase their inspirational achievements first hand to a panel of leading industry figures.

original text:
Junior high is where things really start to happen. Cliques form and break apart. Couples are made and destroyed. And a reputation is solidified that you won't ever be able to escape. Everything you do and say, and everyone you spend your time with, matters.
Katie Mills knows that. She gets it. That's why she tried so hard to get in with the cool girls at school. And why she was so devastated when those efforts found her detained for shoplifting and laughed out of cheer squad tryouts.
But Katie has more to worry about than just fitting in. Her parents are divorced and always fighting. Her sister never has time for her. And her friends all seem to be drifting apart. Even worse? The boy she has a crush on is dating the mean girl at school.
Everything is a mess, and Katie doesn't feel like she has control over any of it. Certainly not over her weight, which has always topped out at slightly pudgier than normal - at least, according to her mother.
So when she happens to catch one of the popular girls throwing up in the bathroom one day, it sparks an idea. A match that quickly engulfs her life in flames.
Is there any going back once she gets started down this path?
And would she even want to if she could?
Born and raised in a suburb of Milwaukee, K. J. Farnham was a teacher for 12 years and became a freelance writer in 2012. She holds a bachelor's degree from UW-Milwaukee and a master's degree from Carroll University in Waukesha, Wisconsin. A sequel to "Click Date Repeat" is in the works, and she plans to publish her next novel, "Don't Call Me Kit Kat," in the spring of 2015.

Couples are made and destroyed.

She gets it.
That's why she tried so hard to get in with the cool girls at school.
And why she was so devastated when those efforts found her detained for shoplifting and laughed out of cheer squad tryouts.
But Katie has more to worry about than just fitting in.

And her friends all seem to be drifting apart.
Even worse?
The boy she has a crush on is dating the mean girl at school.

Certainly not over her weight, which has always topped out at slightly pudgier than normal - at least, according to her mother.
So when she happens to catch one of the popular girls throwing up in the bathroom one day, it sparks an idea.
A match that quickly engulfs her life in flames.
Is there any going back once she gets started down this path?
And would she even want to if she could?
Born and raised in a suburb of Milwaukee, K. J. Farnham was a teacher for 12 years and became a freelance writer in 2012.
She holds a bachelor's degree from UW-Milwaukee and a master's degree from Carroll University in Waukesha, Wisconsin.
A sequel to "Click Date Repeat" is in the works, and she plans to publish her next novel, "Don't Call Me Kit Kat," in the spring of 2015.

original text:
The honor, given annually by the Center for Bioethics and Culture, recognizes those who have made outstanding contributions to the field of bioethics scholarship and who defend the dignity of humankind and embrace ethical biotechnology for the human good in the face of 21st century challenges. The award is named after Paul Ramsey, a longtime professor of religion at Princeton University and one of the most important figures in medical bioethics of the 20th century. He died in 1988.
"I'm honored to be given an award named for Paul Ramsey, one of the founding figures in the field of bioethics, whose thoughts on many of the issues we face today are still insightful and salient," said Sulmasy, who is also associate director of the MacLean Center for Clinical Medical Ethics.
An ethicist and practicing physician, Sulmasy studies end-of-life decision-making, ethics education and spirituality in medicine. In addition to serving on the editorial boards of several academic journals, he is the editor-in-chief of the journal, Theoretical Medicine and Bioethics. He previously served as chair of the Ethics Committee of the Society of General Internal Medicine and on the Board of Directors for the American Society for Bioethics and Humanities.
He is the author or editor of six books: The Healer's Calling (1997), Methods in Medical Ethics (2001; 2nd ed. 2010), The Rebirth of the Clinic (2006), and A Balm for Gilead (2006). Forthcoming in 2014 are: Safe Passage: A Global Spiritual Sourcebook for Care at the End of Life, and Francis the Leper: Faith, Medicine, Theology, and Science.
"Dr. Daniel Sulmasy demonstrates the spirit of the Paul Ramsey Award, with his exemplary achievements and contributions in the field of bioethics," said Jennifer Lahl, RN, MA, president of the Center for Bioethics and Culture. "We are pleased with his selection."
Past winners of the Ramsey Award include Leon R. Kass, the Addie Clark Harding Professor Emeritus of Social Thought at the University of Chicago, who was a recipient in 2010.
Common among many past winners of the award has been serving on presidential commissions on bioethics. Sulmasy is currently a member of President Barack Obama's Presidential Commission for the Study of Bioethical Issues. Kass was chairman of President George W. Bush's President's Council on Bioethics from 2001 to 2005.
Gilbert Meilaender, PhD, an award winner in 2009 and who studied under Ramsey, lauded Sulmasy.
"Daniel Sulmasy is a physician, philosopher and theologian," said Meilaender, professor of theology at Valparaiso University, a former member of Bush's bioethics council and a member of the award's nominating committee. "But these are not so much separate roles he occupies as they are aspects that interpenetrate and enrich his thinking and his writing. It is our good fortune to profit from his unusual mix of training and expertise."

Raff and Rolf the Wolves PDF Crochet Pattern - IraRott Inc.
FORMAT: PDF, 22 pages, 4.45 MB (mobile devices friendly). Language - English. The pattern is written row-by-row using US crochet terms, diagrams, and step-by-step photos (easy to follow). Both styles are included in the pattern: Furry & Non-Furry Wolf Hat, that could be made with or without a tail.
SKILLS: Chain, single crochet, double crochet, half double crochet, slip stitch, increasing, decreasing, working in the round.

original text:
49:39 - Join Ruben and Clara on annual leave as they wander from the Top of the Rock in the Rockefeller Center to Wall Street. Topics include revolving doors, the subway, Halloween bears, Airbnb, Brooklyn, Williamsburg Bridge, ubiquitous horns and sirens, NYPD clothing, Wendy's potatoes, green nooks, and more. Recorded 7th October 2016.
Recorded in New York, New York. Licence for this track: Creative Commons Attribution 3.0. Attribution: Ruben Schade.

original text:
An innovative technology of WPC pellet production with the addition of solid plastic.
Products are made from granulated WPC and are characterized by enhanced durability.
The waste of local woodworking enterprises is used in the production process of WPC.
The GS Composite plant for production of wood-plastic composites is the first facility in Russia to apply the unique technology for manufacturing WPC pellets with the addition of solid plastic.
The GS Composite plant in Ulyanovsk produces granulated WPC and finished products. The plant is the first in Russia to apply technology for producing a composite mixture with significant proportions of solid plastic - polyethylene terephthalate (PET).
This material is widely used to increase the strength of manufactured products: packaging, construction parts, furniture details and construction formwork elements. The innovative production technology was first developed in Moscow at the GS Group research and technology laboratory.
GS Composite  -  an ecological production that uses sawdust from local woodworking companies and PET waste as raw wood material to manufacture import-substituting products. WPC pellet output is at 500 kg/hour; production capacity is up to 2,150 tons of finished products per year.

WPC pellet output is at 500 kg/hour; production capacity is up to 2,150 tons of finished products per year.

4 New Printable Cheese Coupons!
I always love seeing cheese coupons! They tend to go quickly so grab these new printable cheese coupons, so you have them when we see sales come up.
Don't forget: Each coupon can be printed two times per computer.

original text:
Dance classes in jazz, contemporary, ballet, hip hop, musical theatre and commercial by fully qualified and insured teacher.
Jennifer has been dancing from a young age and trained professionally for four years. She has recently completed her BA (Hons) Degree in Contemporary Dance and each year has had her choreography chosen to be showcased in every end of year show while studying at both West College Scotland, Paisley and the Scottish School of Contemporary Dance (The Space), Dundee.
Jennifer has since had works shown in Theatre Royal, Glasgow and the Edinburgh Fringe Festival.
Jennifer has had a lot of experience in performance and teaching. She has taught dance for active schools and many other dance organisations.
As a Mum Jennifer understands the need for a safe, secure and friendly learning environment where fun is the main priority! Come and be a part of a new dance school! Please feel free to contact Jennifer, information provided on this site.
Sorry, but we have no upcoming events for Jennifer Scott Dance. If you know of an event taking place at Jennifer Scott Dance or anywhere else in Renfrewshire tell us about it and we'll list it on the website, for free!
